title: Everytime Attempt
objective: Survive a 2-4 minute run by dodging incoming forecourt hazards, staying under active pump streams to fill your tank, and choosing when to risk high-price boost zones for bigger score multipliers.

controls.outcome: Move the car between pump lanes and avoid incoming hazards like sliding price boards, honking cars, and flying receipts.
Dash forward into the current pump stream to collect fuel faster but with less reaction time.
Retreat to the back row to clear immediate pressure, but the pump pauses and your combo starts draining.

steps: Start each run with an almost-empty tank and one active pump lane.
Hazards stream across the station in escalating waves while the pump only fills when your car stays aligned with the nozzle.
Every few seconds a new 'attempt' begins: pump lanes reshuffle, hazard patterns change, and a glowing surge-price zone appears on one lane.
Driving through the surge-price zone boosts fill speed and score multiplier, but it also spikes the crisis meter and spawns denser hazards.
Miss too many fills or max out the crisis meter and the run ends with your final gallons turned into leaderboard score.
scoring: Score comes from gallons pumped, near-miss dodges, and maintaining an 'Attempt Chain' multiplier. The multiplier rises when you survive consecutive attempt phases without bailing out, and rises faster inside surge-price
tips: Stay near the active nozzle; pure survival scores badly if the tank is not filling.
Use back-row bailouts sparingly because they protect you but bleed combo.
Take surge-price zones only when a hazard wave has a clear gap.
Short lateral swipes are safer than repeated forward dashes once the crisis meter is high.
Audio helps: pump clicks signal safe fill time, rising strings warn that an attempt reshuffle is about to hit.

trendContext.title: Everytime I attempt to fill up my tank lately I experience a small emotional crisis
